parsed.summary: The v0.4.0→v0.4.6 diff introduces several high-risk areas: a new retry subsystem (retry.mjs) with no upper-bound cap on total sleep time, a BigModel error dispatcher that silently swallows unrecognized errors, a glm-client that wraps HTTP calls with retry but may double-count failures, and job-control/tracked-jobs modules that manipulate state files without atomic writes or file-locking—exposing corruption under concurrent CLI invocations. Missing tests for concurrent file access, retry overflow, and the error dispatcher's default path are the strongest reasons not to ship without further hardening.

findings.title: retry.mjs has no total-time ceiling — a series of retriable errors can block the CLI for hours
findings.body: retry.mjs implements exponential backoff with jitter but never caps cumulative sleep time. If the upstream API returns retriable errors continuously (rate-limit, 503, network timeout), the retry loop will keep sleeping and re-attempting until maxAttempts is exhausted. With defaults of, e.g., 10 attempts and base delay 1000ms, total sleep can reach ~17 minutes. A caller that passes a larger maxAttempts (or a loop that re-invokes retry) can stall indefinitely. No test in retry.test.mjs validates total elapsed time or enforces a wall-clock budget.
findings.recommendation: Add a `maxTotalMs` parameter (default e.g. 300000 = 5 min). Before each sleep, check `elapsed + nextDelay > maxTotalMs` and throw a dedicated `RetryBudgetExhausted` error. Add a test that verifies retry aborts when the budget is exceeded regardless of remaining attempts.

findings.title: bigmodel-errors.mjs default dispatch path silently drops unrecognized errors
findings.body: classifyAndDispatch() maps known BigModel error codes to handler functions, but if the error code doesn't match any known pattern, the function falls through to a default path that logs a generic message and returns without re-throwing or escalating. This means any novel error type (new API error code, unexpected response shape) is silently swallowed — the caller sees a successful return with no data, propagating empty/null results upstream without any indication of failure.
findings.recommendation: In the default/fallback dispatch branch, re-throw the error (or throw a wrapped UnknownBigModelError) so callers cannot silently proceed with missing data. Add a test that passes an unrecognized error code and asserts the error propagates.

findings.title: state.mjs and tracked-jobs.mjs write JSON without atomic rename — concurrent CLI runs corrupt state
findings.body: Both state.mjs and tracked-jobs.mjs write state to JSON files using fs.writeFile directly. If two CLI processes run concurrently (e.g., user invokes /glm:review while /glm:status is polling), the writes interleave and produce truncated or merged JSON. There is no file locking, no write-to-temp-then-rename, and no corruption recovery. The state files are read on every command invocation, so corruption breaks all subsequent commands.
findings.recommendation: Replace direct fs.writeFile with an atomic write: write to a .tmp file in the same directory, then fs.rename to the target path. Add a try/catch around state reads that detects JSON parse errors and either recovers from a backup or resets to empty state with a clear warning. Add a test that simulates concurrent writes and verifies no corruption.

findings.title: glm-client.mjs retry integration may hide permanent failures behind transient error classification
findings.body: glm-client.mjs wraps HTTP calls with the retry subsystem, classifying HTTP status codes as retriable or not. However, the classification logic appears to treat any 4xx that isn't explicitly 429 as non-retriable while treating all 5xx as retriable. A 5xx response from a malformed request (e.g., the API returning 500 for an invalid model name) would be retried uselessly, wasting time and potentially hitting rate limits. There's no mechanism to detect 'repeated identical 5xx' as a signal to stop retrying.
findings.recommendation: Track the response body/status of each retry attempt. If the same 5xx error repeats consecutively (same status + same error code in body), treat it as non-retriable after 2 occurrences. Log a distinct warning so users can diagnose permanent misconfigurations masked as transient failures.

findings.title: tracked-jobs.mjs cleanup of stale jobs has no error handling — partial failure leaves orphan entries
findings.body: The tracked-jobs module attempts to clean up stale/expired job entries during state loads, but if the cleanup partially fails (e.g., one entry removed, then a write error), the in-memory state diverges from disk. Subsequent operations may reference job IDs that were already removed from disk, or vice versa. No test covers partial failure during cleanup.
findings.recommendation: Wrap cleanup in a try/catch. On failure, log the error and skip cleanup rather than leaving state in an indeterminate form. Consider making cleanup lazy (remove on next access) rather than batch, to reduce the blast radius of a failed cleanup.

findings.title: No test coverage for concurrent CLI invocation safety
findings.body: The test suite includes unit tests for retry, state, args, render, and bigmodel-errors, but there are no integration tests verifying behavior when two CLI processes run simultaneously against the same state directory. This is a realistic scenario (user runs review in one terminal, status in another) and the state/tracked-jobs modules are not concurrency-safe.
findings.recommendation: Add a test that spawns two concurrent processes performing state mutations and verifies that the final state file is valid JSON containing the union of both processes' writes, or at minimum that neither process crashes with a corrupt state error.

next_steps: Add maxTotalMs ceiling to retry.mjs and ship a test proving the budget is enforced
Change bigmodel-errors.mjs default branch to re-throw unknown errors instead of silently swallowing them
Implement atomic writes (write-to-temp + rename) in state.mjs and tracked-jobs.mjs before exposing to concurrent use
Add smart-dedup detection in glm-client.mjs retry: stop retrying identical consecutive 5xx responses
Add a concurrency smoke test for state file mutations under parallel CLI invocations
